title: Explainers: subgroup/multiplicity, non-inferiority, forest plots & heterogeneity
context: Three trial-design and reporting topics generate persistent misreadings in oncology: (1) post-hoc subgroup analyses are frequently treated as confirmatory when they are hypothesis-generating and multiply-tested; (2) non-inferiority trials are often misread as equivalence trials, and the non-inferiority margin is rarely interrogated; (3) forest plots visualize subgroup HRs and heterogeneity, but the I-squared statistic and the interaction test are rarely interpreted correctly. These are M2 advanced topics that build on ci-pvalue-012 and absrel-nnt-014.
objective: Author, biostatistician-sign-off, and publish explainers on subgroup analyses and multiplicity, non-inferiority and equivalence trials, and forest plots and heterogeneity — covering the mechanism of each potential misreading, how to detect it, and the correct interpretation — with reproducible worked examples on open/synthetic data.
acceptanceCriteria: Subgroup/multiplicity explainer: plain and precise definition of a subgroup analysis; explicit commonTrap covering why post-hoc subgroup findings are hypothesis-generating (multiple comparisons inflate type I error; pre-specified vs post-hoc distinction matters); the interaction test as the correct test for subgroup effects; how many multiple comparisons are expected to yield p < 0.05 by chance.

Non-inferiority explainer: plain and precise definition of non-inferiority and the non-inferiority margin; explicit commonTrap covering: non-inferiority is not the same as equivalence; the margin choice is a clinical judgment that should be interrogated; a non-inferiority result means the new treatment is not worse by more than the margin, not that it is equivalent.

Forest plot/heterogeneity explainer: plain and precise definition of the forest plot, the pooled estimate, and heterogeneity (I-squared, Q statistic, tau-squared); commonTrap covering: I-squared alone does not quantify the magnitude of heterogeneity; visual inspection of whether CIs overlap is not a valid heterogeneity test.
Each topic has a worked example on open/synthetic data with a manifest entry; all figures and numbers emitted by committed scripts and diff-matched by repro-005.
Every conceptual claim cites an authoritative methods source; howToReadInAPaper checklists included for each topic.
Structural 'educational, not clinical advice' framing present; no specific trial verdict stated.
Biostatistician statsSignoff recorded; clinical-framing and accessibility reviews passed.
Zero stated statistical falsehoods; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; DCO signed-off.
resources: Reporting guidelines: CONSORT (subgroup analysis extension), PRISMA (heterogeneity), ICH E9; methods literature on non-inferiority design and forest plots (cited per-claim)
output: Published, biostatistician-signed-off explainers on subgroup/multiplicity, non-inferiority, and forest plots/heterogeneity, with 100% reproducible worked examples, full provenance, all claims cited, accessibility passed, and structural 'not clinical advice' frame.
